nodes.summary: Per-stream snapshot store plus per-side threshold table that gates E1/E2 rollouts by substituting held snapshots when V_s falls low.

nodes.name: shared_latent_probe.py
nodes.summary: MECH-423 R1 readiness instrumentation: a pure function measuring per-module gradient coupling on a shared latent (non-zero grad norms + non-negative inter-module gradient cosine) to detect uncoupled or negative-transfer regimes.

nodes.name: shared_latent_gradient_probe
nodes.summary: Computes per-module gradient norms and mean pairwise cosine on a shared latent, flagging uncoupled or net-negative-transfer conditions.

nodes.name: version_layering.py
nodes.summary: Central registry and guard helpers for higher-version (V4/V5) architecture-epoch flags, enforcing that any V4/V5 substrate change to shared ree_core code defaults OFF so V3 execution stays bit-identical. Enumerates each generation-tagged master flag so parity smokes, contract tests, and the runner preflight can mechanically assert no-op-default safety.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a NamedTuple as an immutable flag record and dotted-path attribute walking (getattr chain) to probe a built REEConfig for drift; treats a missing path as a surfaced drift signal rather than a silent pass.

nodes.name: GenerationFlag
nodes.summary: Immutable NamedTuple describing one V4/V5 master gate on a shared code path: its generation, dotted config path, no-op default value, owning claim id, and a human note.

nodes.name: find_flags_on
nodes.summary: Returns the dotted paths of any registered V4/V5 flag not sitting at its no-op default on a given config, flagging missing paths as drift; an empty result means V3 default behaviour is preserved.

nodes.name: assert_all_off
nodes.summary: Raises AssertionError naming the offending flag paths if any registered V4/V5 flag is not at its no-op default, used by the runner preflight and no-op-default contract test to guard V3-default safety.

nodes.name: iter_generation_flags
nodes.summary: Returns the registered generation flags, optionally filtered to one or more given generations (e.g. v4, v5).

nodes.name: causal_grid_world.py
nodes.summary: Monolithic V3 grid-world environment implementing the SD-005 split body_state/world_state observation contract plus every substrate-lever the REE fleet exercises (proxy-gradient harm/benefit fields, SD-022 limb damage, SD-023 landmark gradients, SD-029 scheduled external hazards, SD-047 multi-source drift, SD-048 interoceptive noise, SD-049 microhabitat zones, SD-054 bipartite reef pools, SD-065 conditioned-safety cue channel, and MECH-353 action-block curriculum). Sole environment consumed by V3 experiment scripts and the CausalGridWorldV2 proxy-mode alias.
nodes.languageNotes: Single ~4.7k-line class with 60 methods and a ~1000-line __init__ that gates ~15 orthogonal SD-* / MECH-* substrate levers behind default-off booleans; bit-identical-when-disabled is a hard project invariant (see NUM_ENTITY_TYPES=7 / 5x5x7 local_view width comments).

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld
nodes.summary: V3 2D causal grid world with persistent agent footprint. Returns split body_state/world_state observations for the SD-005 z_self/z_world encoders and hosts every optional substrate extension (proxy fields, effort dissociation, microhabitat zones, bipartite reef pools, weather drift, interoceptive noise, scheduled hazard/limb-damage injection, action blocks, SD-065 safety-cue channel). Every extension is default-off and MUST leave the observation shape and RNG stream bit-identical when disabled.
nodes.languageNotes: Class-level constants ACTIONS and NUM_ENTITY_TYPES=7 are fleet-invariants; changing them re-shapes local_view (5x5x7=175) and breaks every trained checkpoint.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld.__init__
nodes.summary: Massive ~1050-line configuration constructor: parses every substrate lever (SD-005 split channels, SD-011/012 harm streams, SD-022 limb damage, SD-023 landmarks, SD-029 external hazard, SD-047 multi-source drift, SD-048 interoceptive noise, SD-049 microhabitat zones, SD-054 bipartite reefs, SD-065 safety cue, MECH-353 action blocks, Q-080 effort dissociation), validates pre-conditions (raises when levers require missing prerequisites), and initialises per-episode state buffers.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld.reset
nodes.summary: Per-episode reset. Rebuilds the grid layout (microhabitat zones, bipartite pools, landmark placement), re-seeds resource/hazard/waypoint positions, resets telemetry and effort state, and clears per-episode substrate state (limb damage, safety-cue activation, interoceptive noise). Preserves the manual safety-cue override (deliberate experimenter control) across reset.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld.reset_to
nodes.summary: Deterministic reset that pins agent, hazard, and resource positions from caller-supplied coordinates. Used by fixed-layout probes and reproducibility harnesses where the full randomised reset() would decorrelate from a saved seed / trajectory.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld.step
nodes.summary: The core environment transition (~1150 lines). Executes agent action with SD-022 limb-failure gating and MECH-353 action-block cancellation, applies contamination/footprint dynamics, injects scheduled external hazard (SD-029) and scheduled limb damage (SD-022), computes SD-065 safety-cue activation for the tick, drifts hazards and respawns resources/waypoints, and returns the split obs_dict (body_state, world_state, harm streams, transition_type, telemetry).

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._apply_effort_dissociation_layout
nodes.summary: Q-080 effort-dissociating layout: partitions the grid into a cheap corridor and a costly detour so paths with equal geometric length carry different metabolic cost, letting downstream experiments dissociate effort-conditioned choice from distance-conditioned choice.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._get_observation_dict
nodes.summary: Assembles the per-tick observation dict: body_state (position, health, energy, footprint, heading, progress; optional harm/benefit exposure), world_state (5x5x7 local_view, contamination view; optional hazard/resource/landmark/safety-cue field views), and auxiliary streams (SD-010 harm_obs, SD-011 harm_obs_a with SD-048 interoceptive noise applied, telemetry, transition_type). Materialises the SD-005 split-channel contract that z_self/z_world encoders consume.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._compute_proximity_fields
nodes.summary: Recomputes ARC-024 proximity gradient fields: continuous hazard_field and resource_field arrays whose values fall off with distance to each hazard/resource. These fields are the substrate for the pre-contact harm/benefit signal that HarmForwardModel and E3.harm_eval learn to anticipate.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._place_reef_patches_bipartite
nodes.summary: SD-054 bipartite placement: partitions the interior into two forage half-planes and places reef patches / resources / hazards according to that partition, producing a spatial structure that dissociates side-of-map from resource-type identity.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._build_microhabitat_zones
nodes.summary: SD-049 Voronoi microhabitat construction: draws seed points, expands them into zones, filters out zones that fail to survive coverage/size checks, and records per-zone resource/hazard multipliers. Zones bias resource and hazard spawning while keeping the grid topology intact.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._init_multi_source_state
nodes.summary: SD-047 multi-source initialisation: seeds the weather AR(1) field, transient event scheduler, and background drift velocities used to produce non-stationary, source-decomposable environment dynamics on top of the base grid.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._step_weather_field
nodes.summary: SD-047 weather tick: advances the weather AR(1) Gaussian field one step, producing a slow-drifting scalar innovation classified as irreducibly random (masked from MECH-314b/c novelty when curiosity is on).

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._step_transient_events
nodes.summary: SD-047 transient event scheduler: probabilistically appears and disappears transient hazards/benefits with Poisson gating, returning per-tick appear/disappear counts. Off by default; carries irreducibly-random draws that must be masked from novelty.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._step_background_drift
nodes.summary: SD-047 background drift: advances hazard positions by their maintained drift velocities, respecting bounds and collisions, so hazards move even in the absence of any agent-driven event. Position drift is observable and Markovian (classification (a)).

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._apply_interoceptive_noise
nodes.summary: SD-048 interoceptive noise: injects a fatigue AR(1) drift, a sensitisation Poisson onset, and an autonomic i.i.d. standard-normal jitter into the harm_obs_a affective stream. Off by default; when on it is the primary irreducibly-random source that inflates E3 prediction error to expose MECH-314b/c novelty gating failures.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._inject_external_hazard
nodes.summary: SD-029 scheduled external hazard: on the configured cadence, relocates one hazard adjacent to the agent (optionally strictly adjacent) so the environment guarantees externally-caused hazard events per seed, giving the SD-029 action-outcome comparator enough SNR to distinguish self-caused from world-caused harm.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld.set_safety_cue
nodes.summary: SD-065 test-phase API for the conditioned-safety cue channel: sets a tri-state manual override (None follows the schedule; True/False force on/off) that takes precedence over the safety_cue_on_relief pairing path and persists across reset. Enables presenting the cue concurrently with a threat for the MECH-304 promote-to-active behavioural falsifier.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._inject_scheduled_limb_damage
nodes.summary: SD-022 direct limb-damage injection (MECH-302 unblock): on the configured cadence, adds a magnitude to one or all limbs regardless of agent action, guaranteeing periodic damage-accumulation to heal trajectories so the SufferingDerivativeComparator has detectable suffering signal even under a trained avoidance policy.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._inject_counter_evidence
nodes.summary: Injects periodic counter-evidence events (hazard-marked cell that turns out safe, or vice versa) to prevent trivially-consistent world models from calcifying and to sustain non-zero PE that gates plasticity downstream.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._drift_hazards
nodes.summary: Legacy hazard-drift routine: on env_drift_interval ticks and with env_drift_prob, relocates one hazard by a single random step, respecting occupancy. Kept for backwards compatibility with pre-SD-047 experiments where non-stationarity was expressed only through simple hazard drift.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._place_biased_near_resources
nodes.summary: Landmark placement biased toward already-placed resources so SD-023 landmark gradients spatially coincide with reward, letting agents that read landmark fields exploit them as reward proxies.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._compute_landmark_field
nodes.summary: SD-023 landmark gradient computation: builds a per-cell scalar field that decays with distance from each landmark position, providing the landmark_a_field / landmark_b_field views included in world_state.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._build_bipartite_pools
nodes.summary: SD-054 bipartite pool builder: partitions available spawn cells into two disjoint pools by forage half-plane / zone type, applies interior/agent-band filters, and returns the (pool_a, pool_b) tuple the placement routines draw from.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld._spawn_transient_benefit
nodes.summary: Infant-substrate GAP-3 transient-benefit spawn: picks a free interior cell and materialises a short-lived benefit token that decays after a bounded number of ticks, giving agents contingent brief opportunities that fade if not acted on.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorld.render
nodes.summary: ASCII text render: lays out the grid with entity glyphs, agent position, and the hazard-field value at the agent for diagnostic printing. Used by dry-runs and interactive smoke tests; not on any training hot-path.

nodes.name: CausalGridWorldV2
nodes.summary: Convenience alias returning CausalGridWorld with use_proxy_fields=True (V2 proxy-gradient mode: body_obs_dim=12, world_obs_dim=250). Docstring is the canonical INF-ENV-004 stochastic-attractor audit index for the module, mapping every RNG call site to its Markovian-vs-irreducible classification for MECH-314 curiosity gating.

nodes.name: test_phase3_runpack_materialize.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Phase-3 sync_daemon run-pack materialization pipeline that backfills per-run run-pack directories (manifest.json / metrics.json / summary.md) from flat evidence JSON, exercising the golden byte-shape against a canonical EXQ-633 pack, backfill/skip logic, non-eligible filtering, field-mapping provenance rules, and the default-off feature flag.
nodes.languageNotes: unittest.TestCase suite with a per-test temporary git repo (via subprocess `_git` helper) and a `_make_flat` fixture-builder that fabricates flat manifest dicts matching the sync_daemon field-mapping contract.

nodes.name: _make_flat
nodes.summary: Test-fixture helper that builds a minimal flat manifest dict (run_id / experiment_type / architecture_epoch / claim_ids / outcome / criteria) matching the sync_daemon field-mapping contract.

nodes.name: GoldenByteShape
nodes.summary: Bit-identical golden test asserting that runpack_for_flat applied to the EXQ-633 flat manifest reproduces the on-disk EXQ-633 run pack exactly (relative run_dir, manifest bytes, metrics bytes, summary text).

nodes.name: MaterializeRunpacks
nodes.summary: End-to-end tests for sync_daemon._materialize_runpacks over a scratch git repo: backfills missing run packs and stages them, skips runs whose pack already exists, ignores non-eligible flat files, and processes multiple flat manifests in a single pass.

nodes.name: FieldMappingProvenance
nodes.summary: Tests the flat-to-runpack field-mapping rules: provenance keys are carried into the pack when present, omitted (not nulled) when absent, per-arm aggregates fold in only when no top-level metrics exist, and top-level metrics always win over aggregates.

nodes.name: conditioned_safety_store.py
nodes.summary: SD-051 / MECH-304 substrate: a non-trainable EMA-prototype of z_world captured at MECH-302 relief-completion event ticks; issues a cosine-similarity safety-prediction scalar consumed by the agent to gate avoidance-commitment release (IL->CeA expression pathway). SD-066 centered-readout + MECH-358 escape-affordance bridge safety-signal wiring via the 2026-07-15 predict() amendment.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-Python stdlib arithmetic (no torch, no nn.Module inheritance); accepts torch tensors defensively via getattr/hasattr and coerces to a Python list before per-scalar EMA math.

nodes.name: ConditionedSafetyStore
nodes.summary: EMA-prototype conditioned safety predictor for SD-051 / MECH-304: learns which z_world states predict suffering absence via MECH-302 relief events (encoding pathway) and emits a sigmoid(cosine)-based safety_prediction scalar (expression pathway). Optional SD-066 centered mode subtracts a slow common-mode baseline before matching so a small cue residual can dominate under SD-008 z_world under-differentiation. MECH-094-gated: simulation ticks never advance the prototype.
nodes.languageNotes: Prototype + optional baseline held as plain Python lists updated in place; supports both raw-cosine and SD-066 centered-residue readouts under a single gain-adjusted sigmoid.

nodes.name: __init__
nodes.summary: Configures the safety store: world_dim, EMA / decay / gain / threshold knobs, and the SD-066 centered-mode toggle plus its baseline_alpha. Initialises the prototype vector, the optional common-mode baseline, and internal counters (n_events, has_prototype).

nodes.name: update
nodes.summary: Waking-path tick: coerces the incoming z_world into a plain list, optionally advances the SD-066 common-mode baseline EMA (skipped in centered-mode when sim_mode is set), applies per-step L2 decay toward zero, and on a MECH-302 event tick blends the (centered or raw) z_world into the prototype via ema_alpha. Returns the current safety-prediction scalar via _query. Under sim_mode returns 0.0 with no state change (MECH-094).
nodes.languageNotes: Defensive tensor coercion (detach/squeeze/tolist) keeps the class torch-free while accepting torch inputs; L2 decay + EMA implemented as scalar list comprehensions.

nodes.name: _query
nodes.summary: Internal read: returns 0.0 when the prototype norm is below min_norm (no learned cue yet); otherwise computes cosine similarity between the query vector and the prototype and maps it through a scaled sigmoid (gain * cos) to produce the safety_prediction scalar in [0,1].

nodes.name: predict
nodes.summary: Read-only prediction API added 2026-07-15 for SD-059 / MECH-358: coerces z_world into a list, applies SD-066 centering when enabled (without mutating the baseline), and returns _query(...) as the safety signal fed into the escape-affordance bridge. Never advances prototype or baseline state.

nodes.name: reset
nodes.summary: Clears the prototype and baseline back to zero vectors and resets n_events / has_prototype for a fresh developmental episode without re-instantiating the store.

nodes.name: module.py
nodes.summary: V3 HippocampalModule — CEM-style trajectory proposer over action-object space, ghost-seeded imagination, replay/reverse-replay/diverse-replay, per-region V_s streams, invalidation broadcast, goal payload build, and SD-024 representational-density delegate + SD-025 curiosity-drive scoring.
nodes.languageNotes: Single 2400-line class encapsulating the whole hippocampal loop; heavy torch tensor plumbing, many private helpers (_score_trajectory, _curiosity_bonus, _propose_ghost_seeded) plus MECH-269/287/288/292/293/339/340/357/358 wiring.

nodes.name: field.py
nodes.summary: V3 ResidueField over z_world — RBF-based harm/benefit/safety accumulators with per-center bandwidth (SD-024 density modulation), EWC anchoring, valence priority, and coverage telemetry; the substrate's ethical-terrain memory.
nodes.languageNotes: Two tightly-coupled torch.nn.Module classes (RBFLayer + ResidueField); RBFLayer adds SD-024 add_residue_cluster / compute_local_density; ResidueField adds SD-052/066/067 safety-terrain fields and SD-024 compute_benefit_density.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule
nodes.summary: The V3 hippocampal core — CEM planner over action-object space with ghost-seeded proposals, replay/reverse/diverse-replay, per-stream V_s bookkeeping, invalidation broadcast, event segmentation, staleness accumulation, anchor-set ticking, and goal payload assembly.

nodes.name: RBFLayer
nodes.summary: Per-center-bandwidth RBF layer over z_world used to accumulate harm/benefit/safety residue attractors and repellers; adds SD-024 add_residue_cluster (spatially-graded co-events) and compute_local_density (DA-modulated density readout).

nodes.name: ResidueField
nodes.summary: Persistent ethical-terrain memory over z_world — three RBF fields (harm, benefit, safety) with EWC anchoring, valence-priority arbitration, and coverage telemetry; enforces the MECH-094 hypothesis-tag invariant that simulated content cannot accumulate residue.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.__init__
nodes.summary: Constructs the hippocampal module — instantiates EventSegmenter, AnchorSet, StalenessAccumulator, GhostGoalBank, InvalidationTrigger, the E2Fast forward reference, ResidueField reference, per-region V_s tables, and the ghost-goal ranking / curiosity-drive state used by SD-025.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule._score_trajectory
nodes.summary: Scores a candidate trajectory by combining residue-shaped terrain cost with the SD-025 curiosity bonus (higher curiosity for unfamiliar z_world regions) — the score used by the CEM elite-selection pass.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule._curiosity_bonus
nodes.summary: SD-025 curiosity term — computes an inverse-familiarity bonus over a proposed world sequence so unfamiliar regions are up-weighted during CEM scoring.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.update_familiarity
nodes.summary: Waking-phase familiarity update (SD-025) — increments the internal familiarity tracker for actually-visited z_world regions so subsequent proposals experience decaying novelty.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.propose_trajectories
nodes.summary: Main CEM proposer over action-object space — runs elite-scored iterative refinement, injects support-preserving candidates, mixes value-flat proposals with ghost-seeded ones, and returns ranked trajectories plus diagnostics.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule._propose_ghost_seeded
nodes.summary: Ghost-seeded proposer branch — draws imagination candidates from GhostGoalBank ranking (MECH-292/293) and folds them into the CEM population as prior-shaped starts.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.replay
nodes.summary: MECH-092 quiescent replay — samples valence-weighted start states and replays trajectories through E2Fast with hypothesis_tag=True (MECH-094 forbids residue accumulation).

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.reverse_replay
nodes.summary: Backward replay of a completed trajectory used for MECH-287-style credit assignment — walks the trajectory in reverse under hypothesis_tag.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.diverse_replay
nodes.summary: Diversity-weighted replay — draws exploration-tagged trajectories with an entropy-penalising sampler so replay covers arousal-diverse states.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.compute_representational_density
nodes.summary: SD-024 representational-density delegate — asks ResidueField for local RBF-center density at a z_world query so DA-modulated density can steer allocation.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.record_committed_trajectory
nodes.summary: Records a trajectory the agent actually committed to acting on — feeds the backward-credit sweep and per-stream V_s update pipeline.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.backward_credit_sweep
nodes.summary: Assigns outcome-quality credit back over the last committed trajectory — updates per-stream V_s and returns a diagnostics dict consumed by governance.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.update_per_region_vs
nodes.summary: Per-region V_s update pass — decays region values by staleness, applies incoming credit, and reconciles with the event-segmenter's region roster.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.apply_invalidation_broadcasts_to_regions
nodes.summary: Fans out InvalidationTrigger broadcasts into per-region V_s streams — the read path for z_world staleness events (MECH-339/340).

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.build_goal_payload
nodes.summary: Assembles the goal payload passed onwards to E3 / policy — merges ranked trajectories, ghost-goal candidates, anchor-set probes, and diagnostic telemetry.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.tick_anchor_set
nodes.summary: MECH-269 anchor-set tick — advances the anchor proposer/prober one step, culls stale anchors, and returns the fresh anchor-vs-probe telemetry.

nodes.name: HippocampalModule.forward
nodes.summary: torch.nn.Module forward entry — thin dispatcher that routes to propose_trajectories and returns the ranked trajectory bundle used by the agent's outer step loop.

nodes.name: RBFLayer.forward
nodes.summary: RBF forward — computes per-center Gaussian responses over z with per-center bandwidth (SD-024) and returns a scalar residue field value.

nodes.name: RBFLayer.add_residue
nodes.summary: Adds a single RBF center at the given location with an initial intensity — returns the new center index for downstream valence updates.

nodes.name: RBFLayer.add_residue_cluster
nodes.summary: SD-024 spatially-graded cluster write — deposits a set of correlated RBF centers around an anchor location with per-center bandwidth tuned by local density, so co-events form graded fields rather than isolated spikes.

nodes.name: RBFLayer.compute_local_density
nodes.summary: SD-024 density readout — counts / weights active RBF centers within an effective radius of a query z_world and returns a density scalar consumed by the DA-modulated allocation pathway.

nodes.name: RBFLayer.update_valence
nodes.summary: Updates the signed valence of an existing RBF center — used by ResidueField.update_valence to promote / demote residues after outcome resolution.

nodes.name: RBFLayer.evaluate_valence
nodes.summary: Evaluates the valence-weighted field at z — Gaussian-mixture of per-center valence used by ResidueField.evaluate_valence for priority arbitration.

nodes.name: ResidueField.__init__
nodes.summary: Constructs the ResidueField — instantiates the harm RBFLayer plus optional benefit (ARC-030/MECH-117) and safety (SD-052/066/067) RBFLayers, sets EWC anchoring state, and wires valence-priority thresholds.

nodes.name: ResidueField.ewc_penalty
nodes.summary: Computes the elastic-weight-consolidation penalty against the last snapshot_ewc_anchor — used to keep learned residue centers from drifting during downstream training.

nodes.name: ResidueField.evaluate
nodes.summary: Evaluates the harm residue field at a z_world query — thin wrapper delegating to the harm RBFLayer.forward pass.

nodes.name: ResidueField.accumulate
nodes.summary: Writes a new harm residue after action execution — refuses when hypothesis_tag=True (MECH-094: simulated content cannot accumulate residue) and folds world_delta (SD-003) with harm_magnitude.

nodes.name: ResidueField.evaluate_benefit
nodes.summary: ARC-030 / MECH-117 benefit-terrain read — evaluates the benefit RBFLayer at z_world; higher means closer to a previously-beneficial region (liking channel).

nodes.name: ResidueField.accumulate_benefit
nodes.summary: Writes a benefit-terrain attractor at resource-contact / approach events — the write path for the liking channel; obeys the same MECH-094 hypothesis-tag guard as the harm path.

nodes.name: ResidueField.compute_benefit_density
nodes.summary: SD-024 benefit-density readout — delegates to the benefit RBFLayer's compute_local_density so the DA-modulated allocation pathway can distinguish benefit-dense from benefit-sparse regions.

nodes.name: ResidueField.evaluate_safety
nodes.summary: SD-052 / SD-066 / SD-067 safety-terrain read — evaluates the safety RBFLayer at z_world; positive value means a previously-safe region, feeding hippocampal terrain scoring.

nodes.name: ResidueField.accumulate_safety
nodes.summary: SD-052 / SD-067 safety-terrain write — deposits a safety attractor when a relief-paired safety cue is observed; supplies the substrate needed by the MECH-303/304 falsifier pipeline.

nodes.name: ResidueField.discharge_domain
nodes.summary: Domain-scoped discharge pass — decays or gates a subset of residue centers (harm / benefit / safety) according to domain-specific rules used by the coverage-management pipeline.

nodes.name: ResidueField.integrate
nodes.summary: Runs a small number of integration steps over the residue fields — used to advance state during quiescent windows and returns per-field diagnostic statistics.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for ree_core.regulators; re-exports the regulator-layer substrates (SD-036 GABAergic decay, MECH-287 invalidation trigger, SD-037 broadcast override, SD-069 phasic surprise burst, MECH-282 LPB interoceptive routing, MECH-295 liking bridge, MECH-319 simulation-mode rule gate) under a single flat import surface.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package __init__ acting as a re-export barrel; defines an explicit __all__ so `from ree_core.regulators import X` resolves the seven submodule public symbol groups.

nodes.name: sync_daemon.py
nodes.summary: The sole git writer for Phase-3 coordination data on the hub VM: hosts the result-manifest, queue-snapshot, and heartbeat writer loops that read the coordinator SQLite database and commit under phase3:/phase3-queue:/phase3-heartbeats: prefixes to REE_assembly and ree-v3. Contains extensive env-var validation, writer-health telemetry, active-claim gating, and non-fast-forward / conflict recovery machinery designed to be safe under concurrent writers.
nodes.languageNotes: Very large single-module daemon (~2820 lines, 48 top-level functions, no classes) built on subprocess-driven git plumbing, SQLite reads, and an ephemeral private-index commit pattern to avoid working-tree race conditions.

nodes.name: test_phase3_queue_writer.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for sync_daemon.phase3_queue_writer that build seeded bare-git remotes and ree-v3 clones to exercise happy-path materialization, terminal-item exclusion, idempotency, dirty-tree / foreign-commit refusals, calibration-field round-tripping, reconcile upsert-only mode, non-fast-forward conflict recovery, and no-self-deadlock behaviour after failed commits or fetches.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses unittest with per-test tempdir bare-remote+clone fixtures and monkeypatch injection of subprocess.run to simulate flaky git operations.

nodes.name: reconcile_once
nodes.summary: Reconciles the coordinator DB against the on-disk experiment_queue.json snapshot: upserts every queue item into the experiments table and, unless upsert_only is set, deletes rows for queue_ids no longer present in the file (the legacy pre-Phase-3 destructive mode).

nodes.name: _load_queue_json
nodes.summary: Loads a queue JSON file from the working tree, preferring `git show HEAD:<path>` under REE_LOAD_QUEUE_FROM=head so writers do not race with concurrent working-tree edits, falling back to a direct file read.

nodes.name: _validate_batch_size
nodes.summary: Parses and range-checks a PHASE3_*_BATCH_SIZE env var, returning the supplied default when the value is missing, non-integer, or out of the [1, 256] band.

nodes.name: _validate_branch_name
nodes.summary: Validates a branch-name env var (non-empty, no whitespace) and falls back to a configured default on any violation.

nodes.name: _validate_abs_repo_path
nodes.summary: Validates an absolute repo-path env var (non-empty, no whitespace, starts with '/'), falling back to a default when malformed.

nodes.name: _validate_repo_relpath
nodes.summary: Validates a repository-relative path env var, rejecting absolute paths, paths starting with './' or '../', and any '..' segment traversals.

nodes.name: _validate_float
nodes.summary: Parses a positive float env var and falls back to the default when missing, non-numeric, or non-positive.

nodes.name: _validate_bool
nodes.summary: Parses a boolean env var recognising the standard true/false/1/0/yes/no/on/off tokens (case-insensitive) and falls back to the default on any other value.

nodes.name: _record_writer_commit
nodes.summary: Records a successful writer commit into the in-process writer-health table, tagging it with the resolved short SHA and commit subject for later /writer-health readouts.

nodes.name: _record_writer_conflict_recovery
nodes.summary: Increments the per-writer conflict-recovery counter recording that the queue writer self-healed a rebase conflict on experiment_queue.json.

nodes.name: _persist_writer_health
nodes.summary: Atomically writes the current writer-health snapshot (pid, per-writer timers, pending-spool count) to a temp file and os.replace()s it into the target path for the /writer-health coordinator endpoint to serve.

nodes.name: _bootstrap_writer_health_from_git
nodes.summary: On daemon start, walks each writer's target repo history for its expected commit prefix and seeds the in-memory writer-health record with the most recent commit's SHA / timestamp so /writer-health does not show a spurious never-committed state before the first tick fires.

nodes.name: _extract_heartbeat_machine_state
nodes.summary: Projects a machine's DB status document and heartbeat payload into the small canonical state dict (current EXQ, status, phase, host_time_utc) the state-change detector compares across ticks.

nodes.name: _phase3_heartbeat_is_silent
nodes.summary: Returns True when a machine has not sent a fresh heartbeat within the configured stale window (used to decide whether to emit a 'going silent' state-change event).

nodes.name: _phase3_heartbeat_compute_changes
nodes.summary: Diffs the previous per-machine committed state against the freshly extracted current state and produces the list of state-change events (start / finish / switch / idle / silent / restart) the heartbeat writer commits on; state-change-only semantics is how the writer stays off the git-history-bloat path.

nodes.name: _phase3_heartbeat_phrase
nodes.summary: Renders a single heartbeat state-change event into a human-readable subject-line phrase (e.g. 'machine started V3-EXQ-XXX', 'machine went silent') for the phase3-heartbeats: commit message.

nodes.name: _phase3_heartbeat_commit_message
nodes.summary: Assembles the phase3-heartbeats: commit subject from a list of state-change events, collapsing to a summary form when many events land in the same tick.

nodes.name: _fsync_dir
nodes.summary: Opens a directory and calls os.fsync on its file descriptor so a preceding os.replace() rename is durable across a crash; portable across platforms that reject directory fsync.

nodes.name: _revert_target_to_head
nodes.summary: Reverts a single working-tree path to its HEAD content via `git checkout HEAD -- <path>`, unlinking the file first when the path is not present in HEAD; used to clean up a failed writer's staged output without touching sibling paths.

nodes.name: _git
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around subprocess.run for `git -C <repo> <args...>` with configurable timeout and check semantics; the single git invocation shim every writer uses.

nodes.name: _maybe_revert_exclusive_telemetry_dirt
nodes.summary: Best-effort auto-cleanup: when the hub tree is dirty ONLY on files inside the heartbeat writer's guarded prefixes (runner_heartbeats/, runner_status/), reverts them to HEAD so the phase3 writers can proceed rather than refuse-on-dirty-tree; landed after the 2026-06-01 hub-wedge incident.

nodes.name: _hub_working_tree_clean
nodes.summary: Returns True when `git status --porcelain` shows an empty working tree; the primary guard the phase3 writers use before committing to avoid picking up unrelated staged files.

nodes.name: _restore_derived_path
nodes.summary: Materialises a specific relative path from HEAD via `git checkout HEAD -- <relpath>` to recover files that HEAD carries but the working tree lacks (the HEAD/worktree-skew hazard from CLAUDE.md), reporting each recovered path via stderr.

nodes.name: _hub_working_tree_clean_for_writer
nodes.summary: Composite guard used by the heartbeat writer: first attempts the exclusive-telemetry auto-revert, then returns the tree-clean check so a writer can decide whether to proceed.

nodes.name: _active_claim_blocks_writer
nodes.summary: Reads TASK_CLAIMS.json under REE_Working and returns True when any active claim lists a resource path that overlaps the writer's guarded prefixes (evidence/, docs/claims/, or the queue file); the guard that prevents phase3 writers from silently reverting a live session's uncommitted edits.

nodes.name: _check_ahead_writer_authored
nodes.summary: Walks the commits between origin/<branch> and local HEAD and returns True only when every ahead commit's subject starts with one of the writer's own prefixes; used to distinguish a writer-authored ahead state (rebase-safe) from a foreign commit (refuse-and-log).

nodes.name: _sync_to_origin
nodes.summary: Runs a fetch and, when local is behind origin, rebases writer-authored ahead commits onto the refreshed base; refuses on foreign ahead commits, and (when PHASE3_QUEUE_CONFLICT_RECOVERY is on) self-heals a queue-writer rebase conflict via `reset --hard origin/<branch>` + re-materialise-from-DB so the writer resumes on the next tick.

nodes.name: _load_runpack_builder
nodes.summary: Imports the REE_assembly build_run_pack module lazily (inserting its scripts/ path onto sys.path) and returns its build function used by the result-writer to materialise per-run runs/<run_id>/{manifest,metrics,summary} packs.

nodes.name: _materialize_runpacks
nodes.summary: For each staged manifest, delegates to build_run_pack to produce the runs/<run_id>/ pack, atomically writes each file (temp + fsync + rename + directory fsync), stages it with `git add`, and reverts partially-written files on any exception so the writer never commits a half-materialised pack.

nodes.name: _materialize_sidefiles
nodes.summary: Copies each pending sidefile from the manifest spool into the target evidence directory next to its parent manifest with the same atomic-write + fsync + git-add + revert-on-error discipline used for runpacks.

nodes.name: phase3_git_writer
nodes.summary: The result-manifest writer: drains the coordinator's on-disk manifest spool, writes each manifest to its evidence path, materialises the runpack and sidefiles, commits the batch under phase3: with a hub private-index commit, and pushes to origin with foreign-commit guards and best-effort conflict recovery.

nodes.name: _canonicalise_queue_item
nodes.summary: Normalises a queue-item dict to the canonical JSON key order the on-disk experiment_queue.json requires so an unchanged DB state produces a byte-identical file (the idempotency guarantee the queue-writer relies on).

nodes.name: _materialise_queue_from_db
nodes.summary: Reads all non-terminal experiments from the coordinator DB, canonicalises each into a queue item, and merges the current calibration block from the working-tree file so a queue-writer tick emits the full, calibration-preserving snapshot.

nodes.name: phase3_queue_writer
nodes.summary: The queue-snapshot writer: rebuilds ree-v3/experiment_queue.json from the DB (preserving the calibration block), commits under phase3-queue: when the file differs from HEAD, and optionally self-heals a rebase conflict via reset+re-materialise so the writer converges on the next tick.

nodes.name: _atomic_write_text
nodes.summary: Atomically writes text to a path via a temp file next to the target, fsync, rename (os.replace), and directory fsync so a mid-write crash cannot leave a torn file on disk.

nodes.name: _safe_machine_filename
nodes.summary: Sanitises a machine hostname into a git-safe filename (strip whitespace, replace unsafe chars, cap length) used to name the per-machine runner_heartbeats/*.json and runner_status/*.json files.

nodes.name: phase3_heartbeat_writer
nodes.summary: The heartbeat/status writer: reads per-machine live status from the coordinator DB, detects state-change events against the last committed state, atomically writes the two per-machine JSON files, and commits under phase3-heartbeats: only on real changes (with the 2026-06-23 liveness-tick retired to cut git-history bloat).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Daemon entry point: parses env-configured writer intervals + branch/path settings, opens the coordinator DB, seeds writer-health from git history, then loops calling reconcile_once and each enabled phase3 writer on its own tick cadence while persisting writer-health snapshots for /writer-health.

nodes.name: _QueueWriterFixture
nodes.summary: Base TestCase fixture that spins up a temp-dir SQLite DB plus a bare git remote and seeded ree-v3 clone containing experiment_queue.json, and provides helpers for invoking phase3_queue_writer and reading the local vs origin queue snapshots.

nodes.name: StubGate
nodes.summary: Contract test asserting the default admission-gate stub returns False so no writer accidentally executes without the caller enabling it explicitly.

nodes.name: HappyPath
nodes.summary: End-to-end happy-path test: seeds the DB with a pending queue item, runs the writer, and asserts the canonical file is written to the working tree, committed, and pushed to the bare origin.

nodes.name: TerminalItemsExcluded
nodes.summary: Verifies the writer drops completed/failed experiments from the on-disk queue snapshot so only actionable pending items remain in experiment_queue.json.

nodes.name: IdempotentNoop
nodes.summary: Asserts a second writer tick with an unchanged DB produces no new commit and no push (idempotency: the canonicalisation must produce a byte-identical file).

nodes.name: DirtyTreeRefusal
nodes.summary: Asserts the writer refuses to run when the working tree is dirty on unrelated files, preventing it from sweeping in another session's staged changes.

nodes.name: ForeignCommitRefusal
nodes.summary: Asserts the writer refuses to push when local is ahead of origin with a foreign (non-writer-prefixed) commit, avoiding overwriting an operator's manual queue edit.

nodes.name: CalibrationRoundtrip
nodes.summary: Verifies the writer preserves the existing calibration block in experiment_queue.json across ticks so the operator-owned calibration data is never dropped by a DB-only materialisation.

nodes.name: ReconcileUpsertOnly
nodes.summary: Regression tests for reconcile_once's upsert_only mode confirming stale DB rows survive (safe under concurrent writers) while the legacy default-mode still deletes stale rows.

nodes.name: ConflictRecovery
nodes.summary: Tests the PHASE3_QUEUE_CONFLICT_RECOVERY self-heal path: fabricates a diverging origin edit + a local writer-authored ahead commit, then asserts the flag-on path resets, re-materialises, and lands the DB view while the flag-off path refuses and the foreign-commit rejection still holds.

nodes.name: NoSelfDeadlockOnFailedTick
nodes.summary: Failure-recovery tests using a failing pre-commit hook and a flaky-git monkeypatch to verify the writer leaves the working tree clean after a failed commit or failed post-write fetch and successfully recovers on the next tick.

nodes.name: e3_selector.py
nodes.summary: E3 trajectory selector implementing V3 harm evaluation, ARC-016 dynamic precision commit gating, and the full basal-ganglia analogue selection stack including ARC-107 constitution (MECH-448 F-eligibility demotion, MECH-449 Go/No-Go), ARC-108 learned dopamine-gated w_chan, MECH-450 W_lat recurrent settling, ARC-110 segregated cortico-BG-thalamic loops, and MECH-439 F-dominance conflict-grade levers.
nodes.languageNotes: Extremely large single-class module (3343 lines for E3TrajectorySelector with 42 methods) housing the arbitration substrate; deliberately extended in place per ARC-106 G2 reuse-before-duplicate guidance.

nodes.name: SelectionResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass carrying the trajectory selection outcome: chosen trajectory, index, first action, per-candidate scores, dynamic precision, commit flag, and optional log-probability for REINFORCE.

nodes.name: project_channel_range
nodes.summary: Range-preserving deterministic projection that turns a per-candidate feature representation into a per-candidate scalar bias via centred SVD, used by the modulatory-bias-selection-authority route-range amend so channel-level cross-candidate variation reaches the E3 accumulator.

nodes.name: E3TrajectorySelector
nodes.summary: Central E3 arbitration engine: scores CEM trajectory candidates over z_world via harm/goal/residue heads, applies dynamic precision commit gating (ARC-016), composes the modulatory bias chain, and runs the full basal-ganglia selection stack (F-eligibility demotion, Go/No-Go, learned channel gating, lateral settling, disinhibitory soft-competition, segregated loops with ascending spiral, conflict-graded shortlists).
nodes.languageNotes: Registry of ARC-108/MECH-451 channel names + softplus-unity init constants defined at module scope; the class houses 42 methods spanning scoring, gating, learned three-factor updates, segregated-loop arbitration, and diagnostics without a nn.Module inheritance for the learned buffers.

nodes.name: OPERATOR_GUIDE.md
nodes.summary: Operator guide for the Phase-3 shadow coordination service, covering the Explorer coordination panel, mental model of writers and daemons, phased cutover from git-as-IPC to sync_daemon, and troubleshooting of writer wedges, telemetry drift, and daemon code drift incidents.
nodes.languageNotes: Long-form operational runbook with 24 sections spanning coordination panel semantics, phase transitions, and post-mortem-style incident notes with concrete queries and remediation commands.

nodes.name: actor_critic.py
nodes.summary: First-class actor-critic action-learning primitive (MECH-457): dorsal-striatal-analog actor with a Categorical over discrete actions plus optional plain, successor-feature, or distributional critic heads, architecturally distinct from lateral_pfc/OFC E3 bias-head REINFORCE readouts.
nodes.languageNotes: PyTorch nn.Module with dataclass step output; supports three critic modes (scalar value head, successor-feature via reward-weight vector, distributional value bins) selected by config flags.

nodes.name: ActorCriticStep
nodes.summary: Lightweight dataclass bundling one actor-critic decision step: sampled action index, log-probability, scalar value estimate, entropy, and diagnostic tensors (logits, successor phi/psi, distributional value logits).

nodes.name: ActorCriticPolicy
nodes.summary: Actor-critic policy nn.Module with shared MLP trunk, softmax policy head, and a switchable value critic (plain scalar / successor-feature reward-weighted / distributional two-hot bins); provides forward, critic loss, deterministic-or-stochastic action selection, and actor-parameter enumeration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses torch.distributions.Categorical for stochastic sampling; ValueBins integration realises Dabney-2020-style distributional value estimation with HL-Gauss/two-hot projection.

nodes.name: agent.py
nodes.summary: REEAgent — the central V3 substrate composing E1/E2 world/self/harm predictors, PFC, amygdala, hippocampal, PAG, sleep, residue, comparators, and safety modules into a single tick-driven cognitive agent with candidate-based action selection, offline sleep passes, and multi-loss online learning.
nodes.languageNotes: PyTorch-based; extremely large single-class agent (~9.4k lines) wiring 40+ project-internal modules; heavy use of injected diagnostic signals and mode-gated (wake/SWS/REM) execution paths.

nodes.name: candidate_support_preflight
nodes.summary: Preflight check that a candidate-action tensor is well-formed (shape, dtype, finiteness) before it enters the selector, so bad candidate batches fail loudly instead of poisoning downstream selection stats.

nodes.name: AgentState
nodes.summary: Lightweight container carrying the per-tick agent state snapshot (mode, latents, gating flags) that is passed between substrate modules and returned to callers.

nodes.name: REEAgent
nodes.summary: Central agent class: owns every substrate module (predictors, PFC, amygdala, hippocampal, PAG, sleep, residue, safety, governance) and exposes the tick API (sense -> select_action -> record_transition -> update_*), offline sleep passes, and the online multi-loss training surface.

nodes.name: REEAgent.select_action
nodes.summary: Central action-selection loop: samples candidate trajectories, applies infralimbic/BLA/OFC gating, harm/suffering rejection, curiosity/novelty priors, and picks a final action with logprob and diagnostic bookkeeping.

nodes.name: REEAgent.__init__
nodes.summary: Instantiates all substrate modules (E1/E2 predictors, hippocampal, PFC, PAG, amygdala, sleep, residue, comparators, safety) and wires their config, RNG, and cross-module handles.

nodes.name: REEAgent.sense
nodes.summary: Encodes an observation into latent streams (world, self, safety, harm, resource) and updates buffers, theta packets, comparators, and BLA context features for the tick.

nodes.name: REEAgent.reset
nodes.summary: Resets per-episode state: buffers, comparators, exploration episode, gating flags, and hippocampal/PFC session state while preserving learned parameters.

nodes.name: REEAgent.update_z_goal
nodes.summary: Updates the goal latent z_goal from PFC/hippocampal signals, applying maintenance, decay, super-ordinal write gating, and goal-completion detection.

nodes.name: REEAgent.update_residue
nodes.summary: Refreshes the residue field after a transition: care/simulation markers, decay, cross-stream binding, and mood/regulated-baseline updates.

nodes.name: REEAgent._assemble_control_vector
nodes.summary: Composes a top-down control vector from neuromodulator plane states (5HT/DA/NE proxies), gating masks, and sleep/wake mode for downstream module use.

nodes.name: REEAgent._update_blocked_agency
nodes.summary: Updates the BlockedAgency affect module from harm/suffering derivatives, escapability estimates, and injected go/nogo signals.

nodes.name: REEAgent._e1_tick
nodes.summary: Runs one deep-predictor (E1) forward step: consumes theta packets, updates deep world/self model, and emits prediction targets.

nodes.name: REEAgent.run_sws_schema_pass
nodes.summary: Executes an SWS (slow-wave sleep) offline pass: schema consolidation, replay from ghost-goal bank, and prediction-loss reduction against archived transitions.

nodes.name: REEAgent._e3_tick
nodes.summary: Runs one E3 selector/diversity step: computes candidate scores, diversity bonuses, and updates E3 selector state for the current tick.

nodes.name: REEAgent._update_scientist_attribution
nodes.summary: Updates the scientist-attribution buffer by attributing observed outcomes to regions and adjusting confidence/discovery bookkeeping.

nodes.name: REEAgent.run_rem_attribution_pass
nodes.summary: Executes an REM offline pass: attribution learning, cross-stream binder training, and residue field re-integration under REM neuromodulation.

nodes.name: REEAgent.update_schema_wanting
nodes.summary: Updates the schema-wanting signal from cue recall and benefit salience; drives approach-oriented candidate scoring in select_action.

nodes.name: REEAgent.compute_resource_identity_loss
nodes.summary: Computes the resource-identity encoder loss: matches encoded resource features against identity labels for grounding.

nodes.name: REEAgent.compute_prediction_loss
nodes.summary: Aggregates E1/E2 world-model prediction losses over the transition buffer for the online training step.

nodes.name: REEAgent.compute_harm_accum_loss
nodes.summary: Loss for the harm-suffering accumulator: fits accumulator dynamics against observed harm signals.

nodes.name: REEAgent.notify_env_completion
nodes.summary: Handles environment episode-completion: flushes exploration episode, updates task-outcome bookkeeping, and triggers offline-integration checks.

nodes.name: REEAgent.compute_event_contrastive_loss
nodes.summary: Event-contrastive loss over the theta packet stream: separates same-event vs different-event latents to sharpen segmentation.

nodes.name: REEAgent.compute_harm_nonredundancy_loss
nodes.summary: Non-redundancy loss between the two harm predictors (harm_a and harm_s) so they learn dissociable harm sub-signals.

nodes.name: REEAgent._candidate_world_summaries
nodes.summary: Rolls candidate trajectories through the E2 world model and returns per-candidate summary stats used by the selector.

nodes.name: REEAgent._curiosity_candidate_summaries
nodes.summary: Computes per-candidate curiosity/novelty signals used as exploitation-amplifier priors in select_action.

nodes.name: REEAgent.cue_recall_wanting
nodes.summary: Cue-driven recall path that lifts wanting from the hippocampal cue-recall bank; substrate for MECH-303-style resource seeking.

nodes.name: REEAgent.compute_resource_proximity_loss
nodes.summary: Resource-proximity encoder loss: keeps resource encoder features monotonic in a physical proximity signal.

nodes.name: REEAgent._flush_exploration_episode
nodes.summary: Flushes the current exploration episode buffer into stored trajectories and resets per-episode exploration state.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Central REE configuration hub aggregating 16 dataclass config schemas (Latent/E1/E2/E3, Hippocampal, Residue, Heartbeat, Environment, sleep/anchor/ghost-goal subsystems) and the master REEConfig with the load-bearing REEConfig.from_dims factory that surfaces virtually every substrate flag through one call site.
nodes.languageNotes: Very large single-file config surface (~6988 lines, 16 dataclasses, 700+ fields). REEConfig.from_dims is the intentional single entry point wiring every no-op-default substrate flag through one signature.

nodes.name: LatentStackConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass configuring the shared latent stack (z_self / z_world / z_beta / z_theta / z_delta) including split-encoder dims, reafference (SD-007), resource encoder (SD-015), harm-stream (SD-010/SD-011), z_harm_un (SD-019a), and multi-content theta packet (MECH-294) knobs.

nodes.name: E1Config
nodes.summary: Dataclass configuring the E1 deep predictor (LSTM/schema readout, SHY normalisation, SD-016 frontal cue-indexed integration, cue-slot tagger, ContextMemory diversification loss).

nodes.name: E2Config
nodes.summary: Dataclass configuring the E2 fast predictor (world/self forward models, ARC-033 harm forward, SD-056 action-conditional contrastive loss + multi-step rollout stability, cross-stream binding, uncertainty head).

nodes.name: E3Config
nodes.summary: Very large dataclass configuring the E3 trajectory selector: precision/urgency gating (ARC-016), score-bias composition, modulatory selection authority + channel routing + shortlist top-k, MECH-341 diversity, MECH-448 F-eligibility demotion, MECH-449 Go/No-Go, ARC-108 learned channel gating, MECH-450 W_lat settling, MECH-451 finer channels, ARC-110 loop segregation.

nodes.name: EventSegmenterScaleConfig
nodes.summary: Per-scale configuration for the MECH-288 event segmenter (PE-threshold or BOCPD-Gaussian change-point detector over selected latent streams).

nodes.name: EventSegmenterConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level MECH-288 event segmenter config bundling per-scale detector configs, boundary-event emit targets, and hierarchical scale-id formatting.

nodes.name: InvalidationTriggerConfig
nodes.summary: MECH-287 invalidation-trigger config: broadcast gain, downstream targets, and phasic/tonic guardrail (tonic threshold + rolling window) that suppresses over-broadcasting.

nodes.name: AnchorSetConfig
nodes.summary: MECH-269 anchor-set config: registered scales, reset/hysteresis thresholds, staleness decay, dual-trace capacity, and SD-039 payload feature flag.

nodes.name: StalenessAccumulatorConfig
nodes.summary: MECH-284 region-indexed staleness accumulator config: leak factor, attribution mode (equal or stream_overlap), clamp, and drop epsilon.

nodes.name: GhostGoalBankConfig
nodes.summary: MECH-292 ranked ghost-goal bank config plus MECH-339 composite-cue outshining and MECH-340 persistence/efficacy gating.

nodes.name: PersistenceAppraisalComputeConfig
nodes.summary: Small config for computing MECH-340 persistence appraisals (efficacy / unattainability) from prior completion + commitment + goal proximity.

nodes.name: HippocampalConfig
nodes.summary: Umbrella hippocampal-module config: SP-CEM proposer (ARC-065), MECH-293 ghost-goal probes, per-stream/per-region V_s (MECH-269 phases), MECH-269b rollout gating, anchor set, staleness, event segmenter, invalidation trigger, MECH-292 bank, MECH-290 backward credit sweep, MECH-340 persistence gate.

nodes.name: ResidueConfig
nodes.summary: ResidueField config: RBF layer sizes, SD-014 valence-vector enablement, SD-024 DA-modulated benefit density (cluster allocation + bandwidth narrowing), SD-052 contextual safety terrain, EWC anchor + lambda for MECH-334 crystallization write-protect.

nodes.name: HeartbeatConfig
nodes.summary: Heartbeat / clock config: multi-rate periods (E1/E2/E3), beta-gate bistable + hippocampal completion release threshold, MECH-090 R-c commit-entry readiness conjunction (score-margin gate + single-candidate policy).

nodes.name: EnvironmentConfig
nodes.summary: Environment-facing dims used to build downstream configs (obs shapes, action dim, harm shapes). The seam REEConfig.from_dims reads to size latent + predictor + selector configs.

nodes.name: REEConfig
nodes.summary: Master REEConfig dataclass and factory: hundreds of substrate flags spanning every subsystem cluster (SD-032 cingulate, SD-034 closure, SD-036/SD-037 regulators, SD-049 multi-resource, MECH-090/091/092 control plane, ARC-108/110 loop-segregation learned gating). from_dims is the single load-bearing factory that surfaces the no-op-default flag surface and composes nested dataclass configs from environment dims.
nodes.languageNotes: REEConfig is the single from_dims entry point every experiment builds an agent through -- new substrate flags must be surfaced here or downstream getattr fallbacks silently keep them off.

nodes.name: phasic_surprise_burst.py
nodes.summary: SD-069 LC-NE phasic-surprise regulator that injects event-locked, exponentially-decaying temperature deltas onto the E3 softmax as the phasic complement to MECH-313's tonic noise-floor. Amended by SD-075 (2026-07-19) to add per-episode EMA baseline-continuity (carry vs reset) and accounting-only warmup gating so short-episode seeds no longer measure event count as a function of episode length.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure-arithmetic regulator (no nn.Module, no learned params); dataclass config + stateful class with EMA baseline, geometric envelope decay, and bit-identical OFF via master switch.

nodes.name: PhasicSurpriseBurstConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding the ten no-op-default knobs that parameterise PhasicSurpriseBurst: EMA decay for the surprise baseline, trigger ratio/floor, phasic temperature delta, envelope decay, minimum-temperature floor, excess saturation, SD-075 baseline_continuity ('carry'|'reset') and warmup_ticks accounting knobs.

nodes.name: PhasicSurpriseBurst
nodes.summary: Stateful phasic-surprise burst regulator: per-tick EMA baseline on a surprise signal (running_variance or instantaneous_pe), event-detection when surprise crosses trigger_ratio x baseline, geometric envelope decay driving a temperature delta on the E3 softmax. SD-075 amend adds carry/reset EMA-baseline-continuity across reset() and accounting-only warmup gating (n_events_converged vs n_events_prewarmup) so short-episode phasic experiments read against a converged baseline.

layers.name: Environment
layers.description: CausalGridWorldV2 substrate -- the training environment that ree_core agents observe and act on.

layers.name: Latent Stack + Predictors
layers.description: Core latent representations (z_self / z_world / z_beta / z_theta / z_delta, theta packets, cross-stream binder, self-recurrence) and their forward predictors (E1 slow, E2 fast/world/harm-a/harm-s/uncertainty, E3 selector with score-diversity).

layers.name: Hippocampal + Residue
layers.description: HippocampalModule (anchors, event segmenter, ghost-goal bank, staleness accumulator, persistence-appraisal) and the ResidueField.

layers.name: Cingulate + Salience
layers.description: SD-032 cingulate cluster (dACC, AIC, pACC, PCC, stuck-state detector), salience coordinator, amygdala BLA/CeA analogues, plus the SD-049 per-axis-drive helper feeding the cingulate consumers.

layers.name: PFC
layers.description: Prefrontal-cortex analogues: LateralPFC (SD-033a), OFC (SD-033b), Frontopolar (SD-033e), InfralimbicAvoidanceGate (SD-058), EscapeAffordanceBridge / linker / trainable learner (SD-059).

layers.name: Policy + Selection
layers.description: REEAgent orchestrator (agent.py), goal-representation subsystem (goal.py), umbrella REEConfig, and the action-selection stack: GatedPolicy (ARC-062), CandidateRuleField (ARC-063), noise floor / structured curiosity / tonic vigor / commit-readiness / maintenance-release / rho ramp / natural-commit-urgency / model-disagreement / noisy-selection-head, actor-critic action-learning (MECH-457), difficulty-gated proposal entropy.

layers.name: Regulators
layers.description: Cross-cutting regulators and guardrails: InvalidationTrigger (MECH-287), GABAergicDecay (SD-036), BroadcastOverride (SD-037), LPB interoceptive routing, PAG freeze gate, phasic surprise burst (SD-069), MECH-295 liking bridge, simulation-mode rule gate, VS rollout gate, serotonin neuromodulation plane, beta-gate / clock heartbeat, SD-034 closure operator, and the V4/V5 version-layering guardrails.

layers.name: Sleep + Consolidation
layers.description: SleepLoopManager (SD-017) with sleep-onset gate, BayesianAggregator (MECH-275), SelfModelAggregator (MECH-273), ReplaySampler (MECH-285), RoutingGate (MECH-272), CrossModuleConsolidator + shared-latent probe (MECH-423), MELConsumer (SD-MEL).

layers.name: Attribution + Affect
layers.description: Scientist-agent counterfactual attribution buffer (MECH-276), blocked-agency signal (MECH-353), harm-suffering accumulator (MECH-219), object-file buffer (MECH-045), TPJ / suffering-derivative comparators, conditioned safety store.

layers.name: Coordinator
layers.description: Hub-side Phase-3 coordination infrastructure: pure-stdlib HTTP app, SQLite persistence, manifest spool, sync_daemon writers (results / queue / heartbeats), preflight + verification tooling, PHASE3 test suite, operator/plan/soak documentation, and the schema.sql tables backing the coordinator DB.

tour.title: REEAgent Orchestrator
tour.description: Start at `ree_core/agent.py`, the ~9.7k-line REEAgent that composes every brain-region analogue in the substrate: the latent stack, the E1/E2/E3 predictor cascade, the hippocampal proposer, the cingulate/PFC/policy stack, amygdala + affect, sleep, and the coordination hooks. `goal.py` sits next to it, holding the superordinate goal, the incentive-token drive bank, and the live goal-state vector that every downstream module reads. Read the constructor and per-tick step to see the shape of the wiring — every subsequent step of this tour dives into one submodule that agent.py already wires up.
tour.languageLesson: PyTorch orchestrators of this scale trade class-inheritance for composition: REEAgent owns each submodule as an attribute and calls their forward methods in a fixed per-tick order. Read the top-level step() method to reconstruct data flow rather than tracing base classes.

tour.title: The Training Environment
tour.description: `CausalGridWorldV2` is the single environment every V3 experiment runs against. It implements the SD-005 split-observation contract — body_state (agent-internal) vs world_state (external) — and exposes every substrate lever the falsifier-driven programme needs: harm/benefit regions, safety terrain, controllability injections, curriculum knobs, and the observability configurations that appear in dozens of MECH-* / ARC-* claims. Before reading any predictor you need to know what body_state and world_state look like, because that split propagates all the way through the latent stack.

tour.title: The Latent Stack (L-space)
tour.description: SD-005 splits the agent's latent code into two streams — `z_self` (body / interoceptive) and `z_world` (external) — plus their joint `z_gamma`. `latent/stack.py` houses the encoders, the harm forward heads, the adversarial split head, and the theta-buffer plumbing that every downstream module reads from. The `theta_buffer` (MECH-089) provides the cross-rate integration that lets slower modules see rolling averages of a fast-changing world, and the `cross_stream_binder` installs a genuine shared latent cause between self and world so the split does not degenerate into two independent streams.

tour.title: Predictor Cascade: E1 Slow + E2 Fast
tour.description: The predictor tier implements REE's hierarchical forward-model stack. `e1_deep.py` is the LSTM-based slow world model predicting jointly over `[z_self, z_world]`; `e2_world.py` is SD-031's fast world-state predictor whose residual on the *taken* action serves as the MECH-256 single-pass agency comparator. Two harm heads (`e2_harm_a` for affective pain — MECH-258 — and `e2_harm_s` for sensory-discriminative harm — ARC-033) run alongside, and `e2_world_uncertainty` (SD-063) supplies the distribution-free uncertainty envelope that E3 later reads. Together this cascade produces the prediction errors that drive residue accumulation, hippocampal event segmentation, and the E3 selector's harm scoring.

tour.title: E3 Selector — The Arbitration Engine
tour.description: `e3_selector.py` is REE's basal-ganglia analogue: it scores candidate trajectories on the harm + benefit landscape supplied by the residue field (next step) and the predictor cascade (Step 4), and then arbitrates through the ARC-107 F-eligibility envelope, the MECH-448 / MECH-449 Go/No-Go gate, and the ARC-108 learned dopamine-gated `w_chan`. `e3_score_diversity` (MECH-341) preserves entropy across candidates so the selector doesn't homogenise onto a single strategy. This module is the pivotal decision point every experiment reads from — most falsifier runs turn on some feature of the E3 arbitration flow.

tour.title: Hippocampal Memory + Imagination
tour.description: `hippocampal/module.py` is a CEM-style trajectory proposer over the action-object space, seeded with ghost goals from `ghost_goal_bank.py` and threaded through replay / reverse-replay / diverse-replay for offline learning. `event_segmenter.py` uses prediction-error thresholding and Bayesian online changepoint detection to cut experience into discrete events, which is what makes replay coherent. This is where the substrate's imagination happens — every candidate trajectory the E3 selector scores in Step 5 was proposed here.

tour.title: Residue Field: Harm / Benefit Landscape
tour.description: `residue/field.py` maintains an RBF-based accumulator over `z_world` for harm, benefit, and safety valences, with per-centre bandwidth (SD-024 density modulation), EWC anchoring against catastrophic forgetting, and valence bookkeeping. It's the falsifier target for a large family of `INV-*` and `MECH-*` claims about care, residue-as-care-marker, and mood regulation. The E3 selector reads this field directly to score candidates; the hippocampal proposer uses it to bias imagination toward informative regions of state space.

tour.title: Cingulate: Salience & Adaptive Control
tour.description: The cingulate stack coordinates the substrate's operating mode. `salience_coordinator.py` (SD-032a) binds the five cingulate subdivisions into a soft operating-mode probability vector with hysteresis-gated switching, and `dacc.py` (SD-032b) holds the precision-weighted affective-pain prediction error (MECH-258) plus recency/monostrategy suppression that flags when the agent is stuck. Downstream PFC and policy modules read these mode signals to decide when to switch strategies rather than persist.

tour.title: PFC Subregions: Rule + Task-State Biasing
tour.description: REE splits prefrontal function across analogues: `lateral_pfc_analog.py` (SD-033a) holds a gated rule-state vector and projects a per-trajectory top-down bias into E3 scoring; `ofc_analog.py` (SD-033b) carries an explicit task-structure `state_code` latent (MECH-261 write-gated) that projects a per-candidate top-down bias. `infralimbic_avoidance_gate.py` (MECH-357 / SD-058) suppresses PAG-driven freezing and adds instrumental override. Together these modules are the top-down half of the arbitration described in Step 5 — the E3 selector's scores are always biased by whatever rule + task-state signal the PFC is currently maintaining.

tour.title: Policy Commitment Machinery
tour.description: REE's policy tier isn't just an action selector; it's a commitment substrate. `gated_policy.py` (ARC-062) implements the weak-reading rule-apprehension layer with two symmetry-broken scoring heads and a learned context discriminator that gates between them. `commit_readiness.py` (MECH-090) is a pure-arithmetic EMA producing a [0,1] motor-program readiness signal, and `commit_maintenance_release.py` (MECH-342) is the release-side complement — together they implement enter/hold/release for a motor program. This is the machinery that turns E3's chosen trajectory into a sustained committed action.

tour.title: Regulators + Amygdala Arousal
tour.description: The regulator layer sits above the commitment machinery and modulates system-wide behaviour. `broadcast_override.py` (SD-037) is the orexin-analog producing a scalar [0,1] override from drive-level and sustained-threat magnitude; `invalidation_trigger.py` (MECH-287) subscribes to boundary events and re-broadcasts invalidations across the substrate. The amygdala analogues (`bla.py` for basolateral, `cea.py` for central) consume the affective harm stream from Step 4 and emit arousal + fast defensive signals that gate the freeze/escape machinery.

tour.title: Slow Affect: Suffering + Blocked Agency
tour.description: Fast valence from Step 4 gets integrated into slow affective load through `harm_suffering_accumulator.py` (MECH-219), a hysteretic controllability-gated integrator that produces the `z_harm_slow` suffering-load state. `blocked_agency.py` (MECH-353) derives a `z_block` control-failure signal that rises when the agent's actions no longer move the world state as predicted. These slow affective states feed back into cingulate salience (Step 8) and PFC rule-switching (Step 9), closing the loop between prediction error and mode arbitration.

tour.title: Sleep, Consolidation, and Scientist Attribution
tour.description: REE has a first-class offline phase. `sleep/phase_manager.py` (SD-017) implements the deterministic K-episode entry into a canonical sleep cycle (WAKING -> SWS -> PHASE_SW -> ...); `cross_module_consolidation.py` (MECH-423) runs a module-tagged interleaved E1/E2 schedule over replay traces so offline learning doesn't destructively overwrite waking modules. `scientist_attribution_buffer.py` (MECH-276) records counterfactual-backed credit attributions during waking that the sleep-phase Bayesian aggregator (MECH-275) then consumes. `mel_consumer.py` (INV-050) reads accumulated Model Error Load to drive learning-demand sleep pressure. This is where V3 does its structural rewriting of what waking experience meant.

tour.title: Phase-3 Coordinator: DB + Schema + HTTP App
tour.description: The `coordinator/` package is the hub-side infrastructure that keeps the experiment fleet coherent. `app.py` is a pure-stdlib bearer-token-authed HTTP app; `db.py` is the SQLite persistence layer with atomic claim arbitration (BEGIN IMMEDIATE + WAL + conditional UPDATE); `schema.sql` defines the experiments queue, results, heartbeats, and claim_log tables. Claim arbitration is coordinator-authoritative — workers `POST /claim` and only one gets the queue item — so understanding this trio is how you understand why two machines never accidentally run the same experiment twice.
tour.languageLesson: SQLite claim atomicity relies on serialisation + a conditional `UPDATE ... WHERE state='queued'` clause instead of an external mutex. If the row isn't in the expected state at commit time the update matches zero rows and the caller loses the race — no locks, no deadlocks, no leader election.

tour.title: Phase-3 Writers + Operator Guide
tour.description: `sync_daemon.py` is the sole git writer for coordination data on the hub VM: it hosts the result-manifest, queue-snapshot, and heartbeat writer loops that read the coordinator DB and emit `phase3:` / `phase3-queue:` / `phase3-heartbeats:` commits. `manifest_spool.py` is the filesystem spool that persists `POST /result` bytes keyed on run_id so the writer can commit them later without blocking the ingest path. `OPERATOR_GUIDE.md` closes the tour: it's the plain-English walkthrough of the Explorer coordination panel, the phased cutover, and the operator's mental model of how the pieces you just toured actually run in production.
